heading: Quinninup vs Quinns Rocks — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Quinninup, Western Australia and Quinns Rocks, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Quinninup has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Quinns Rocks (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Quinninup or Quinns Rocks safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Quinninup has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Quinns Rocks scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Quinninup scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Quinns Rocks scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Quinninup or Quinns Rocks?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Quinninup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
